metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or operational pain metrics have been documented. The Next_Step field (2026-06-04) references 'competitive positioning against the lower-cost concern', suggesting price sensitivity may be a driver, but no specific cost savings target, cycle-time reduction, error rate, or ROI has been articulated by the buyer. This is appropriate for a 28-day-old Qualified-stage deal but must be developed before advancing to the relevant stakeholder full S2P suite scope (8 modules including Merlin AI) implies broad procurement transformation ambition, yet the business case anchoring that ambition is unbuilt. Next move: discovery call with Han Tetteroo (Head of Procurement) to quantify current procurement pain (cycle time, maverick spend, supplier fragmentation, manual invoice processing) and map those to measurable outcomes.

champion.narrative: Han Tetteroo (Head of Procurement) is the sole contact role, designated Decision Maker. He is engaged (last activity 2026-06-04, 22 days ago) and has ownership of the vendor evaluation process, but there is no evidence yet of advocacy, internal navigation, or access to the Economic Buyer. Next_Step (2026-06-04) explicitly calls out 'building out contacts beyond the CPO', confirming Zycus recognizes the single-threaded risk. Han's role (Head of Procurement) positions him as the operational decision maker and process owner, but at $200k spend and full S2P transformation scale, he is unlikely the final approver. A champion must provide introductions, share internal political dynamics, and actively advocate for Zycus against competitors. Han is 'developing'—he is the key thread, but his strength (access to power, willingness to coach, multi-threading support) is unproven. Next move: ask Han directly for introductions to the CFO (Mark Rutten) and CTO (Mark Van de Vijver), and test whether he will share the competitive landscape and internal approval dynamics.

competition.narrative: Competition is live but unnamed. Next_Step (2026-06-04) states 'vendor evaluation underway', confirming a multi-vendor bake-off, and references 'competitive positioning against the lower-cost concern', signaling that at least one rival is competing on price and Zycus is positioned as higher-cost or premium. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is empty. The 'lower-cost concern' phrasing suggests the rival is a cost-focused or regional player (potentially Ivalua, Jaggaer, or a European niche vendor), but no competitor is named. The Next_Step also mentions 'the Heineken reference to be developed', implying Zycus plans to use a Heineken customer win as competitive differentiation (Swinkels is a family brewery, so a peer brewing reference is relevant). The year-end decision timeline and vendor-evaluation language suggest the competitive field is narrowing but not yet at final-two stage. Next move: ask Han Tetteroo directly who else is being evaluated, what their strengths/weaknesses are from his perspective, and how Zycus is positioned (cost, functionality, AI, integration) against each rival.

identify_pain.narrative: Specific operational or financial pain has not been documented. The full S2P product suite (8 modules including Merlin AI) implies the buyer recognizes procurement fragmentation, manual inefficiency, or supplier-risk exposure, but no buyer-articulated pain statement, pain owner, or urgency driver has been captured. Next_Step (2026-06-04) references 'competitive positioning against the lower-cost concern', which implies price is a pain point or constraint, but this is an inference, not a confirmed pain from the buyer. The 'year-end decision target' suggests a forcing function (likely 2027 contract expiration, budget cycle, or transformation mandate), but the underlying pain (cost overruns, compliance risk, invoice cycle time, maverick spend) remains unconfirmed. Next move: discovery call with Han Tetteroo to surface the specific procurement pain (what is broken today, what is the cost of inaction, why now) and identify the pain owner (is it Procurement, Finance, Supply Chain, or the CTO driving digital transformation).

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, and implementation scoping are entirely undocumented. No MSA, SOW, or InfoSec questionnaire references exist. The Next_Step (2026-06-04) notes that 'partnership and pricing strategy still to be decided internally', which may signal Zycus is considering a partner-led or co-delivery model (the ground-truth note flags this as 'PARTNER-LED and/or APAC'), but the buyer-side paper process is unmapped. With an enterprise brewery (Swinkels Family Brewers) and a full S2P suite, legal and InfoSec review will be mandatory gates. Next move: during EB/DM discovery, ask Han Tetteroo what the contracting and approval process looks like (legal, procurement, InfoSec, IT integration review) and whether a partner/SI is involved on their side.

economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is not identified. Next_Step (2026-06-04) explicitly states 'identifying the decision maker and exec sponsor' as a current focus area, confirming the gap is known. No CFO or C-level sponsor has been engaged. Mark Rutten (CFO) is a known account-level contact but has no documented involvement in this opportunity. With a $200k S2P transformation, budget authority likely sits at CFO or Chief Transformation Officer (Mark Van de Vijver, also on account). The sole contact role, Han Tetteroo (Head of Procurement), is the operational Decision Maker but unlikely controls commercial approval at this spend level. Identifying and engaging the EB is the critical next gate before progressing past Qualified. Next move: ask Han Tetteroo directly who will approve the budget and procurement transformation investment, and secure an introduction.

decision_process.narrative: A partial read exists. Next_Step (2026-06-04) states 'vendor evaluation underway with a year-end decision target', establishing a timeline milestone (approximately December 2026, six months from now) and confirming a multi-vendor evaluation is in flight. However, the approval chain, gate structure (RFI → RFP → shortlist → demo → commercial → contract), number of vendors in the running, and internal sign-off requirements are unknown. The opportunity is 28 days old and single-threaded (Han Tetteroo only), so the process has not been fully mapped. The presence of a 'year-end decision target' suggests the buyer is working to a forcing function, likely a 2027 implementation or contract-expiration driver. Next move: map the full process with Han Tetteroo—who else is evaluating, what are the gates, who signs off at each stage, and what happens after the year-end decision (contract negotiation, implementation kickoff).

decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are not yet documented. The product scope is expansive (full S2P suite: iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, iRisk), suggesting the buyer has a broad procurement transformation mandate, but the specific evaluation criteria, weighting, or RFP/RFI requirements have not been captured. Next_Step (2026-06-04) references 'vendor evaluation underway' with a 'year-end decision target', implying a structured process exists, but Zycus has not yet obtained or documented the buyer's scorecard, must-haves, or fit criteria. This is a knowledge gap, not hygiene. Next move: request the evaluation framework, RFP/RFI document, or decision scorecard from Han Tetteroo, or co-create a draft BRD to surface their priorities (cost vs functionality vs AI capability vs integration complexity).

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

confidence_signals.summary: No overt cooling signals. Last activity 22 days ago (2026-06-04) is normal for a 28-day-old, partner-led, early-stage enterprise deal. Engagement pulse is LIVE. Next_Step describes active vendor evaluation with year-end decision target. No buyer disengagement, no stall, no ghost risk at this stage.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-05-29, 28 days old. In Qualified stage for approximately 28 days. Close date 2027-03-31 is 278 days out. No stage slippage detected (single modification 2026-06-02). Last activity 2026-06-04, 22 days ago. With 278 days to close and a year-end decision milestone, the current pace is appropriate for early qualification. Stage velocity is healthy for a new enterprise deal.
evidence: Stage matches maturity: Qualified stage with vendor evaluation underway and year-end decision target aligns with 278-day runway (SF Next_Step__c, 2026-06-04)
Amount ($200k) is reasonable for full S2P suite (8 modules including Merlin) at enterprise brewery scale
Single-threaded risk acknowledged in Next_Step: 'building out contacts beyond the CPO' (Han Tetteroo is sole contact role)
Power mapping in progress: Next_Step explicitly calls out need to identify DM and exec sponsor
Partnership and pricing strategy formulation is appropriate for Qualified stage, not a delay signal
No evidence of stall: last activity 22 days ago is normal for partner-led enterprise motion in early qualification
north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage deal with healthy 278-day runway but engagement stalling: 22 days silent, single-threaded with CPO Han Tetteroo only, economic buyer and exec sponsor unmapped, price-sensitive competitive evaluation underway against lower-cost vendor. Multi-threading and champion development urgently needed.

flags: MAP THE COMPETITIVE FIELD in the next discovery call. 'Vendor evaluation underway' confirms a bake-off, but the rivals are unnamed and their positioning unknown. Ask Han directly: who else is being evaluated, what are their strengths from his perspective, and where does Zycus stand today (cost, functionality, AI, ease of implementation). Use that intelligence to sharpen the Merlin AI wedge (negotiation agent, I2O agentic procurement) as the differentiation the low-cost rival cannot match.
best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal (28 days old, Qualified) with appropriate pacing but three high-impact levers must be pulled in the next 30 days to maintain momentum toward the year-end decision milestone.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is in a multi-vendor evaluation against at least one unnamed 'lower-cost concern' (per Next_Step 2026-06-04). The rival is competing on price, positioning Zycus as premium or higher-cost. No competitor is named in Salesforce or documented calls. The 'Heineken reference' development noted in Next_Step suggests Zycus is preparing a peer-brewing customer proof point to counter the cost objection and demonstrate ROI at enterprise scale. Competitive field is early and unresolved; threat level is medium (live evaluation, price pressure, but Zycus has full-suite AI differentiation with Merlin and enterprise-grade credibility). Single strongest current threat: the unnamed lower-cost vendor, because price sensitivity is explicit and Zycus has not yet built the quantified value case to justify premium positioning.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin AI is in product scope (SF Products__c includes Merlin), but AI positioning strength is unknown—no AIS score, no Avoma calls, no documented buyer AI appetite or excitement. The presence of Merlin in a full-suite S2P deal suggests AI capability is part of the evaluation, but whether the buyer values it, understands it, or is prioritizing it over cost is undocumented. Next_Step references 'competitive positioning' but does not call out AI as the wedge.
